content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against 2e54e84499c17f6d5cfafe87ff0d6ee5068770e8 and found three correctness gaps. Verification run: nix develop -c cargo test --features test-stub, nix develop -c cargo clippy -- -D warnings, nix develop -c cargo fmt --check, and nix build all completed successfully.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4555 excludes the active bead from the similarity ownership lookup, and the handler later proceeds to create a new bead at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5310 when no other match is found. That skips the required “fix-current-bead first” policy: a propose_new_bead whose title/scope strongly matches the active bead will still create a duplicate follow-up instead of being treated as in-scope current work. Add an active-bead ownership check before creation and block/downgrade creation when the current bead owns the proposed work.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The core policy order explicitly puts current-bead scope ahead of both existing-bead mapping and new bead creation.
2. src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1471 returns below-threshold propose_new_bead groups without emitting any pending journal event. The new propose_new_bead_pending event is only in handle_propose_new_bead, while the terminal success reconciliation path called at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:308 uses reconcile_proposed_bead_records and silently records only amendments_processed. Below-threshold proposals therefore miss the required structured pending event with current count and threshold.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: Acceptance requires propose_new_bead_pending whenever the threshold is not met; the production reconciliation threshold path currently has no such emission.
3. src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5580 suppresses covered-by-existing comments using only classification, active_bead_id, and run_id. If one run maps two distinct propose_new_bead findings from the same active bead to the same existing bead, the second comment is skipped even when its finding summary/proposed scope differ, losing the audit trail for that downgrade. Include a stable finding/amendment marker or compare the finding/scope before treating the comment as already posted.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The policy says to append a covered-by-existing-bead comment for mapped work; the current idempotence key can collapse unrelated findings.
